name: Geography of India: Soil, Minerals & Natural Resources

sourceRefs.label: 2026_q page 145 Q171-172 (lignite/Neyveli mismatch; Salem iron ore reserves, with answer key)
Tamil Nadu PSC notes / weshineacademy.com - soil types and mineral distribution

researchNotes: Two real hits plus solid web-sourced TN-specific mineral/soil facts. Following the established pattern from the Rivers topic, TN-specific facts are weighted heavily within this all-India clause, since that's what the real evidence actually favors for a TNPSC exam.

reading.intro: Fourth sub-topic of Geography Unit II. As with Rivers, real exam evidence shows a strong Tamil-Nadu-specific lean even within this all-India clause — TN's own soil types, mineral belts, and named deposits come up disproportionately.

qa.question: Real exam: Evaluate — (i) TN excels other states in lignite coal reserves and production (ii) TN has 90% of the country's lignite reserves (iii) the Neyveli lignite field is located in Villupuram district. Which is incorrect?
qa.answer: Only (iii) is incorrect — the Neyveli lignite field is actually in Cuddalore district (historically part of South Arcot), not Villupuram. (i) and (ii) are correct: Tamil Nadu does lead in lignite, holding roughly 90% of India's reserves.

qa.question: Real exam: Which Tamil Nadu district has 482 million tonnes of low-grade Iron ore reserves?
What are the five main soil types found in Tamil Nadu?
qa.answer: Alluvial, Black, Red, Laterite, and Saline soils.

qa.question: What are the characteristics of Alluvial soil, and which TN districts have it?
qa.answer: Fertile, rich in lime/potassium/magnesium/phosphoric acid, porous and loamy, but deficient in nitrogen and humus. Found in Thanjavur, Tiruvarur, Nagapattinam, Villupuram, Cuddalore, Tirunelveli, and Kanyakumari.

qa.question: What minerals are the coastal districts of Tamil Nadu known for, and what are they used for?
qa.answer: Beach sand minerals — ilmenite, rutile, garnet, and zircon. Tamil Nadu also has bauxite, magnesite, and vermiculite deposits, used in aluminium production, fertilizers, and insulation materials respectively.

qa.question: Where is limestone abundant in Tamil Nadu, and what is it mainly used for?
qa.answer: Tirunelveli and Salem districts — a key raw material for cement production, also used in chemical and construction industries.

qa.question: What is Tamil Nadu particularly renowned for in terms of stone/rock resources?
qa.answer: Granite reserves, known for their quality and variety.

questions.prompt: The Neyveli lignite field is actually located in which district (not Villupuram, despite what some sources claim)?

Which soil type is fertile, rich in lime/potassium/phosphoric acid, but deficient in nitrogen and humus?
Beach sand minerals like ilmenite, rutile, garnet, and zircon are characteristic of which part of Tamil Nadu?
Limestone, a key raw material for cement, is abundant in which two Tamil Nadu districts?
Tamil Nadu is particularly well known for which high-quality rock resource?
